players.name: Xeebs
matchupItemFlags: no armor vs a 4-physical enemy comp

notes: Mr_Meat31 (midlane) was alive and ahead in lane — a shove-and-rotate there flips a 1-2 into a numbers advantage.
Goldilocks324 (offlane) was alive but losing lane and pinned — with them stuck across the map, this was the wrong fight to start.
Mr_Meat31 (midlane) was alive and ahead in lane — a shove-and-rotate there flips a 1-3 into a numbers advantage.
Goldilocks324 (offlane) was alive and never joined — get them to the fight and the count changes.
We took Fangtooth in the same window — read it as a trade, not a clean loss.
They traded the fight for Fangtooth across the map — we won bodies but gave the objective.
We opened it a 1-body down (4v5) — the numbers were lost before the fight was.
Mr_Meat31 (midlane) was dead — went down 27s earlier, so this was never a full-strength fight.
Goldilocks324 (offlane) was alive and ahead in lane — a shove-and-rotate there flips a 1-2 into a numbers advantage.
🎮 private (carry) was alive but losing lane and pinned — with them stuck across the map, this was the wrong fight to start.

Goldilocks324 (offlane) was alive and never joined — get them to the fight and the count changes.

Mr_Meat31 (midlane) was alive and ahead in lane — a shove-and-rotate there flips a 1-2 into a numbers advantage.
🎮 private (carry) was alive and never joined — get them to the fight and the count changes.
Their main damage was Legion (43k physical); 1/5 of us itemized armor — that hurt.
Enemy ran 1/5 on or near their meta core — they were off-meta too, the win was there.

draftNote: 1 of five queued off a bottom-two lane: Mr_Meat31 (midlane→offlane).

coaching.headline: This is the win the losses were missing — we fought ON the objectives and our kills bought majors. Objectives 87k-19k, neutrals 26-15.
coaching.team: Same squad, opposite result, and the macro shows why: when fights went down we took the major in the same window. At 10m we gave the fight but took Fangtooth in the window, and by 20m, 26m, 32m we were winning the fights AND holding the objective. We lost the carry lane (Sparrow theirs all game vs Murdock) and won anyway — The Fey mid (5/3/10) and Iggy offlane (7/4/8) carried the tempo, Khaimera anchored the objective damage, and Steel's 12 assists glued it. One thing to keep tightening: at 10m, 24m and 28m the macro still flagged The Fey alive and ahead but not yet at the fight — when he rotated, we snowballed. Keep grouping on the prize and this is repeatable.
coaching.whatShiftedIt: We fought on top of the objectives instead of away from them — every won fight bought Fangtooth/Prime, and we out-objectived them 87k-19k off our mid/offlane tempo even with a losing carry lane. Same plan next time: take the major in the same window the fight is won.

perPlayer.7c8ab99c-31c5-4ea5-a6fa-e0d6417c3f1b: The Fey mid, 5/3/10 — Mr_Meat31's lane vs Legion, with the Wraith Leggings spike ~19m. That lane lead started the Fangtooth/Prime calls. One tighten: the macro flagged Mr_Meat31 ahead-and-absent at the 10m, 24m and 28m fights — rotate a touch earlier off the shoved wave and those go from even to dominant.
perPlayer.16f28434-561f-4f23-9454-40321f4a1ef3: Iggy offlane, 7/4/8 with 28k damage and 25k into objectives — even early then Goldilocks324's from 15 minutes vs Zarus, and that window went to wrecking objectives instead of farming side. That's exactly the convert-the-lead tempo the team kept missing in the losses. Clean game.
perPlayer.a412baf3-dd17-4f89-8ecf-9d0704f3a79e: Khaimera jungle, 8/6/5 and team-high 34k objective damage — Xeebs' all game vs Boris, and Xeebs set the objectives the team grouped onto. Build note: Xeebs skipped armor (Overlord/Basilisk) into a 4-physical enemy comp; pick it up against that draft to tank the late fights better.
perPlayer.a0c393cc-1437-4bdc-8168-64676ce2166c: Sparrow into Murdock was an uphill lane (theirs from the second checkpoint, first tower lost) and 4/3/3 is a fine hold while the team won the map around Cuban Noobster. The core was off-meta (no Storm Breaker/Sky Splitter/Spectra) so spikes came late (Vanquisher ~25m, Imperator ~36m); the standard core spikes harder and would let Sparrow join the objective groups sooner.
perPlayer.10edbf65-457d-48d0-9151-9df5b4fcb40a: Steel support, 2/4/12 — even early then Willy Guggenheim's from 15 minutes vs Narbash, and 12 assists is real enabling that fed the objective snowball. As the lead, this is the call to repeat: group the won lanes onto Fangtooth/Prime and let the kills buy the major.

threats: Heal-stacking: Narbash. Anti-heal is mandatory against this comp — we brought none.
Enemy is 4/5 physical — a single armor item blunts most of their damage; itemize armor on anyone diveable.
synergy: Our engage/CC: Iggy & Scorch Inferno (knockback). Our kills come from landing burst INSIDE that CC window — whoever lands it, the rest must follow up immediately.
We have sustain (Steel) — favor the longer fight where our heals out-grind them.
Strong AoE/teamfight kit — group at choke points and fight on top of objectives.

ourKits.combo: Bramble Patch slow → Harvest Nettles stacks → Untamed Growth DoT → Fly Trap pull ult
ourKits.winCondition: Fly Trap a clumped team and detonate AoE on the pull
ourKits.passive: takedowns spawn growths + grant move speed
ourKits.playWith: engage that clumps for her Fly Trap
ourKits.playAgainst: spread out vs the pull; she's squishy — dive her

ourKits.combo: Oil Spill → Molotov ignite → Flame Turret → Inferno knockback ult
ourKits.winCondition: layer fire zones on a choke/objective and burn the team
ourKits.playWith: CC that holds enemies in his fire
ourKits.playAgainst: don't fight in his zones — flank; kill the turret, dive him

ourKits.combo: Piercing Shot poke → Heightened Senses AS → Hail of Arrows zone → Inner Fire ult
ourKits.winCondition: ramp Relentless + AS and sustained-DPS the front down
ourKits.playWith: heavy peel — she's immobile and pure DPS
ourKits.playAgainst: she has zero escape — hard-engage her and she dies first

ourKits.combo: Bull Rush knockback → Force Shield wall → Shield Slam knockup+stun ult, Shield Bash %hp
ourKits.winCondition: peel the carry with walls/knockups or engage with Shield Slam
ourKits.playWith: a carry he can wall for and peel
ourKits.playAgainst: his Force Shield blocks projectiles — reposition around it; bait Shield Slam

ourKits.combo: Ambush leap → Unleash AS → Cull stun, Invigorate to cleanse+heal
ourKits.winCondition: stick on one target, stun with Cull and out-sustain via Spirit Regen
ourKits.playWith: follow-up burst inside his Cull stun
ourKits.playAgainst: ANTI-HEAL is mandatory — it guts his whole kit; kite the leap and CC through Invigorate

enemyKits.combo: Detection Protocol track → Chrono Swipe → Primeval Roar shield → Ursine Burst grab ult
enemyKits.winCondition: grab a key target out of position and burst them down
enemyKits.playWith: a team that collapses on his grabbed target
enemyKits.playAgainst: his grab ult is the whole threat — bait it / stay spaced; CC during Primeval Roar

enemyKits.combo: Thunk stun → March! speed → Song heal aura → Crash Bang Boom! AoE slow ult
enemyKits.winCondition: engage with Thunk and group-buff the team through fights
enemyKits.playWith: a dive team that rides his speed and heal
enemyKits.playAgainst: dodge/cleanse Thunk; he's low-damage — kill his carry through the aura

enemyKits.combo: Evade in → Barricade block → Spear of Nyr (execute <20%) → Coliseum ult arena
enemyKits.winCondition: trap a target in Coliseum and out-duel with stacked Gladiator
enemyKits.playWith: a team that joins him inside the Coliseum
enemyKits.playAgainst: don't fight him in his arena — disengage; burst through Barricade's window

enemyKits.combo: Static Trap root → Buckshot → Hot Pursuit AS → Long Arm of the Law global snipe
enemyKits.winCondition: trap for the root, then out-range with Shots Fired and finish with the global ult
enemyKits.passive: every 5th basic is a long-range bonus shot
enemyKits.playWith: frontline/peel to protect his low mobility
enemyKits.playAgainst: almost no escape — engage past the trap and he dies

itemTimelineNote: estMin = first minute the role's measured median gold curve covers the cumulative inventory cost (slot order ≈ purchase order) — THEORY, no purchase timestamps in either API

fights.note: caught-out = deaths outside every clustered fight; costs/conversion use a 90s window over timeline majors (older film — no tower times; Fang/Prime/Orb only); itemGap counts participants' items est. online (median-gold model, THEORY)
